title: Guidelines for simulation exercises on early warning systems for weather induced risks
content: Guidelines offering a practical, adaptable framework to design and run simulation exercises that test and strengthen Early Warning Systems, improving decision‑making, coordination, and timely early action.

title: GEOGLAM crop monitor for early warning report: March 2026
content: The March 2026 GEOGLAM Crop Monitor for Early Warning report provides an overview of global crop conditions with a focus on regions vulnerable to food insecurity.

title: Two Internships in Disaster Risk Reduction and Climate Change Adaptation
content: We seek two interns to join our team remotely to support our efforts in addressing global DRR and policy-related challenges.

title: Leading the change: Women shaping a sustainable future
content: Women are not merely beneficiaries of sustainable development; they are designing its blueprints.

title: Global stockpile for health emergency response: A distributed strategy for pandemic preparedness in a post–COVID 19, fragmenting world
content: The paper argues that COVID‑19 exposed major weaknesses in global health preparedness and proposes a distributed, multi‑actor global stockpile system to ensure faster, fairer access to essential medical supplies during future pandemics.

title: Corporate climate adaptation: translating complex societal risks into business as usual
content: The book how responsible business conduct can be integrated into corporate climate adaptation in the mining sector to ensure that private adaptation efforts do not increase social and environmental risks.

title: Rahat: Educational booklet
content: This education booklet was produced in the context of the RAHAT project which focuses on extreme heat vulnerability in informal housing in Jodhpur, western India, where climate change is intensifying temperature extremes.

title: The invisible threat: why indoor heat can be more dangerous than outside temperatures
content: The brief examines how women-led housing adaptations can reduce dangerous indoor heat exposure in informal homes in India.

title: Bridging science and community: exploring pathways to develop community-based lightning early warning systems in Bangladesh
content: The report examines how integrating scientific forecasting with local community knowledge can strengthen community-based lightning early warning systems in Bangladesh to better protect vulnerable rural populations.

title: Somalia country climate and development report
content: The report examines how climate change affects Somalia’s development and economy and finds that stronger resilience and adaptation policies are essential to reduce future economic losses and support sustainable growth.

title: Impact of flood risks on service disruptions of critical infrastructures by the example of the Euskirchen county, Germany
content: Extreme floods can disrupt critical infrastructure and cause cascading failures, and this study offers an integrated GIS-based method to map and reduce those risks in Germany’s Euskirchen County.

title: NZ’s rising house insurance premiums warn of a system under strain
content: Rising home insurance premiums in New Zealand highlight growing pressure on the country’s natural hazards insurance system as climate change, increasing disaster risks, and higher reinsurance costs challenge its long-term affordability and sustainability.

title: Extreme heat disrupting work and sleep for residents of African informal settlements
content: A quarter of people in three African neighbourhoods have been forced to take time off work due to heat stress, research by IIED and Slum Dwellers International reveals.

title: Heat impacts on informal settlements: participatory heat data collection in Kenya, Tanzania and Zimbabwe
content: This issue paper reports the findings of pilot participatory heat data collection in Kenya, Tanzania and Zimbabwe.

title: Livable Pacific cities and towns: Spotlight: Tides of change - Urban resilience across Pacific landscapes
content: The Livable Pacific Cities and Towns report sets out a strategic framework for resilient urbanization, built around four mutually reinforcing building blocks.

title: Livable Pacific cities and towns: Spotlight: Mapping risk, building resilience: Exposure analysis of Pacific urban areas
content: This Spotlight details the results from risk modelling and scientific analyses designed by World Bank staff to identify the exposure at risk (EaR) from critical natural hazards in 38 urban areas in 10 Pacific Island Countries (PICs).

title: South Africa: Making sense of a water crisis after historic floods
content: The City of Johannesburg, South Africa’s economic capital and most densely populated city, is experiencing a water shortage which has seen residents bathing in public in protest.

title: Investing in inclusion: Why we need gender-responsive climate finance
content: Across Asia and the Pacific, change acts as a "threat multiplier," deepening pre-existing inequalities and disproportionately impacting women during disasters.

title: Transforming community disaster preparedness: PHEDM model evaluation in Uttarakhand, India
content: The study evaluates the PHEDM model in two Uttarakhand villages, showing that community‑level training and structured risk assessment significantly improved disaster preparedness, adaptive capacity and emergency response compared with the control village.

title: Network of advanced weather stations helps Maine farmers save time and money
content: University of Maine Cooperative Extension recently completed installation of an advanced weather station in Orono, the third such station in a network of research-grade weather stations designed specifically for agriculture.

title: Most Saharan dust is generated by ‘hidden thunderstorms’ high above the desert
content: In spring, the massive temperature difference between the already-hot Sahara and still-snow-covered mountains in Europe can generate powerful low-pressure systems that sweep dust northwards.

title: Over USD 29 million mobilised to support Madagascar
content: More than USD 29 million has been mobilised to support Madagascar’s response and recovery following tropical cyclones Fytia and Gezani through a combination of pre-arranged financing solutions.

title: Everglades restoration strengthens South Florida’s climate resilience, study finds
content: By combining long-term observations with cutting-edge measurement techniques, the research offers a rare historical and geographic perspective on the role of wetlands in climate mitigation.

title: Tropopause temperature drives tropical cyclone simulation diversity
content: Tropopause temperature biases create major tropical cyclone differences in models; cooler air boosts storm potential intensity, raising global cyclone frequency and hurricanes in experiments.

title: Ice-rock avalanches in a warming Himalaya indicate pathways toward effective preparedness
content: This publication examines two major ice–rock avalanche events to highlight how preparedness can shape disaster outcomes.

title: Financial protection against catastrophic risks: Floods, fires and other major risks
content: This report aims to support government efforts to build financial resilience against catastrophic risks and mitigate the social, economic and financial consequences of unprotected financial exposures.

title: Assessing nature-based flood mitigation measures at the general area of the confluence of rivers Benue and Niger in Kogi state, Nigeria
content: Flood risks at the Niger–Benue confluence are analysed using GIS, remote sensing and hydrological models, showing peak flooding in 2018 and highlighting how nature‑based solutions could strengthen long‑term resilience in Kogi State.

title: UN endorses Global Framework to strengthen disaster-related statistics
content: The 57th UN Statistical Commission endorsed the Global Disaster-Related Statistics Framework (G-DRSF), the first comprehensive global framework to strengthen how disaster statistics are defined, compiled, and used in national systems

title: PLINIVS-based seismic vulnerability mapping during volcanic unrest in campi Flegrei: A replicable DRM model
content: The study maps seismic vulnerability of 12,700 buildings in Campi Flegrei using the PLINIVS method, identifying high‑risk areas and validating results with post‑earthquake data to support disaster‑risk planning during ongoing volcanic unrest.

title: Climate models with high resolution in the ocean can better represent European heat waves
content: High‑resolution ocean models better capture how North Atlantic temperature anomalies trigger European heat waves, improving simulations of atmospheric blocking and heat‑wave patterns, though some biases still remain.

title: Improved estimates of storm water in sewers could help reduce flooding
content: Recent PhD graduate Gabrielle Migliato Marega (CivMin) developed an improved tool for estimating how much water from heavy rains ends up in sanitary sewers.

title: Publication: Restoring the princess: A case study in disaster response, recovery, and resilience from the Sint Maarten Airport terminal reconstruction
content: This case study examines the Princess’s complex journey from destruction to renewal, offering critical insights for airport owners, operators, and regulators in disaster-prone regions and small island states.

title: Collaborative community-based early warning systems – Solutions brief
content: The brief highlights how the Philippine Red Cross has partnered with communities, local leaders, members of Barangay Flood Resilience Committees, and key government stakeholders to strengthen flood early warning systems in the Philippines.

title: Institutionalizing climate resilience in Asia: From localized interventions to systemic integration
content: The core question is no longer whether adaptation projects are implemented, but whether climate risk becomes embedded in governance systems.

title: A new generation of climate scientists warm up to solar geoengineering
content: The U.K. government last year allocated millions of dollars in funding to investigate climate geoengineering. It marked the first time a state funding body had invested significant money in geoengineering research.

title: Forest damage in Europe to rise by around 20 percent by 2100 even if warming is limited to 2°C
content: Forest damage in Europe caused by wildfires, storms and bark beetle outbreaks is projected to increase compared to recent decades under all analysed climate scenarios, according to an international study.

title: World disasters report 2026
content: This report brings together insights to examine how harmful information impacts responses to disasters, public health emergencies, migration and conflict, ultimately providing both evidence and a roadmap for action.

title: Memorandum of Understanding on cooperation in the area of integrated disaster risk management between the European Union and the regions of Latin America and the Caribbean
content: The MoU aims to promote political dialogue and collaboration to strengthen Disaster Risk Management in both Latin America and the Caribbean and the European Union.

title: How climate change may impact some genetic diseases, including certain types of epilepsy
content: Climate change may worsen symptoms of heat‑sensitive genetic variants, making some neurological and epileptic conditions more likely to appear or intensify during heatwaves. Researchers identify “calortypic” genes that raise hidden vulnerability.

title: Epilepsy Society
content: Epilepsy Society is the UK’s leading provider of epilepsy services. Through their cutting edge research, awareness campaigns, information resources and expert care, they work for everyone affected by epilepsy in the UK.

title: Wildfire smoke increases assaults: Evidence from Seattle
content: This study highlights the direct causal link between wildfire-derived air pollution and interpersonal violence in urban environments, focusing on Seattle, Washington, between 2013 and 2023.

title: Continental drying: A threat to our common future
content: This report outlines the accelerating decline in global freshwater availability, described as continental drying, and presents new evidence on where, why, and how this trend is unfolding.

title: WMO Hurricane Committee retires name of Melissa, replaces it with Molly
content: Hurricane Melissa’s name was retired after its catastrophic 2025 impact in the Caribbean, causing 90+ deaths and record‑strength landfall in Jamaica; it will be replaced by Molly.
